title: Chapter Forty: Counter Martial Arts
content:
## JEONG YEON-SHIN

"Dharma power martial arts! Really--!"

Master Won Jong's shocked cry rang across the ruins. His wrinkled eyes trembled.

The Seventh Apostle was more composed. "Easy to handle? It seems to be martial arts at Shaolin's level, just strong against blood techniques. Not enough to overcome the difference in true martial prowess." Her smile widened. "You're still far below me. Cute."

Her form disappeared.

The energy wave that blazed upward was tremendous. This time, Yeon-shin could not react.

Ma Jin blocked it. The air crumpled at the clash of two supreme masters, and terrifying momentum rose from the Radiant Demon Wing Lord's body as he pressed forward. His great sword had the power to push back the Seventh Apostle's hand.

She narrowed her eyes. "So you were betting much on that youngster?"

"Shut up."

"Strike."

One word. The Blood Flame Sect members swarmed in from all directions, surrounding with military formations. Red hair everywhere. Blades from eight directions.

Yeon-shin saw eyes wide with madness. Each red pupil held something beyond human. Murderers. Like those who had come to the Jeong family estate.

Strength entered his sword hand.

He struck upward. One man's blade shattered. He stepped through to the neck. The feeling of catching bone was brief. The distorted trajectory rose into sword technique, and the Desolate Sword began drawing paths of dim light.

*Radiance.*

Light streams flowed like waves. Everything following was sword strikes. The Radiant Sword Style's unbroken flow -- not just the swift technique now, but the full art created at Zhongnan Mountain. He rode over three swords and sent five heads flying without using much power.

They were insane. They threw their bodies at him. Grabbed at his limbs. Scattered coins imbued with true energy like hidden weapons. Blood Swordmasters pressed in from every angle.

"Lightning Flash! This way!" Baek Mi-ryeo's shout was buried under sword-force waves.

*Ma Jin is tied up with the Seventh Apostle. More Blood Swordmasters than blue-levels.* He assessed the battle with the instinct of a group fighter. The blue-levels were holding, but Yeon-shin was the weak point in this formation.

He operated Eternal Blossom Fist's technique in his left hand. His clenched fist burst force waves. The second move, Advancing Thunder, shattered both a crescent wheel and its wielder's skull.

*Still not enough. I can't lead this situation. That's the role of black-levels.*

A swift Radiant Sword Style took another head. He immersed internally. The dharma-inspired martial art that had risen when he grabbed the Seventh Apostle's dagger had subsided again. He could not raise it at will. The chants were not yet concrete.

"Haah--!"

An aged shout. Master Won Jong striking out palm techniques to his left. The old monk's energy waves were modest -- a martial way monk, not a warrior monk. His practical combat skill was limited.

Two Blood Swordmasters moved on Won Jong.

"Can't handle one guy," one muttered.

"Kill the old monk first."

Harming monks was taboo in every era. The Blood Flame Sect did not care. They were already closing in, blades raised without hesitation.

Yeon-shin swept his senses in all directions. He had been separated from the main formation. At this moment, he was the only one who could help.

He stomped the ground. Self-generated wind swept past his eyes. The cold-smiling figures grew rapidly larger. Dim light gathered at the Desolate Sword's edge -- Radiant Sword Style, the precursor rising like the first glow before dawn.

He split the wind.

The strike passed through the first Blood Swordmaster's brow as the man turned. Instant death. The sword did not stop. It clashed with the second, who had somehow prepared.

"What sword force--!"

Bewilderment in the Blood Swordmaster's eyes. Yeon-shin was not relaxed. That the man could speak meant he had energy to spare.

"Master! To the north!"

Following Radiant Demon technique moves, he kicked the Blood Swordmaster's knee hollow. A kick imbued with Fate Defying Scripture's true energy amplification. The man collapsed. Yeon-shin beheaded him and passed by.

Won Jong's eyes were wide. "How could one at that age--"

"It's meaningless. Move."

The battlefield kept expanding. Strong fighters pushed the perimeter outward. They had already left the Xinye White Martial Faction behind and reached the marketplace. Blood Flame Sect members kept surging in.

They recovered two horses from the tavern where Radiant Demon Wing had unpacked. Yeon-shin was not worried about his companions.

*If I can break out, everyone can.* Even Hyeon Won-chang, weakest among the mission group, was a killing-sword master who thrived in melee.

"This way!"

Won Jong followed. They rode hard through the streets. Civilian houses trembled behind locked doors.

*Going back would only be a burden. The Seventh Apostle is beyond me. Avoiding a battlefield where I can't protect myself -- that is helping.*

---

"They don't seem to be chasing," Won Jong said.

"Unless their lightness techniques are faster than horses. And we killed two Blood Swordmasters."

"*You* did."

Silence. Then Yeon-shin drew Henan's geography in his mind. Ma Jin had made him memorise Desolate Sect's maps before the mission.

*We need to cross the river.*

The horses' hooves reached the waterside. A pleasure boat was passing -- a flower boat with an awning, luxuriously built. Young people aboard, wine cups in hand. Warriors standing guard.

"Let's get on that," Yeon-shin said.

"A pleasure boat?"

Won Jong projected his voice with true energy. "Come here." Blunt as a temple bell.

*A martial way monk who did wall-gazing for ten years. Not much worldly experience.* Yeon-shin sighed inwardly.

The boat turned toward them. Ten warriors stood like guards along the rail. Among them, a young lady approached the ship's side.

"The old monk seems to need travel companions."

Around eighteen. Beautiful, adorned to match. The group's centre. Yeon-shin's gaze went not to her face but to the precious sword at her waist.

Energy waves. Martial artists.

"Venerable Monk is focused on his practice and couldn't mind courtesies," Yeon-shin said smoothly. "Please understand."

"What is this fellow saying?" Won Jong turned sharply.

The young lady laughed. "Interesting. Will you come aboard? We can handle a couple horses."

No particularly strong energy. Yeon-shin bowed slightly and stepped onto the ship's side.

The interior was more banquet hall than boat. Those seated exchanged glances. Young people around the lady's age, showing no inclination to stand and pay respects.

She sat and nodded.

"This is a rising talent meeting of the Deep Martial Alliance. My fellow practitioners call me Sword Art Flower."

Deep Martial Alliance.

*Another of the Thirteen Heavens. Demonic path sects.* Yeon-shin's martial world expanded in that instant. He assessed the boat's occupants. No one particularly strong.

*The Deep Martial Alliance's true elites must be elsewhere.*

A young master propped his chin. "You seem to be Desolate Sect. Why are you trying to cross the river in such a hurry?"

"Blood Flame Sect groups running wild nearby. We fled while listening to the monk's dharma talk."

He spoke truth while watching their reactions. Deep Martial Alliance. The Thirteen Heavens cooperated. There was a high chance they already knew about the Xinye County incident.

Indeed.

Strange smiles. The young master grinned. "Desolate Sect wags its tail to the Thirteen Heavens. Interesting."

Laughter from every direction. Not just wealthy children on a boat ride. Dignified sneers filled the space.

"The blood-sucking ghosts worried about a small fry like you fleeing," Sword Art Flower said. "Didn't expect to catch a Shaolin monk too. This happens to be the best place in the world for silencing people."

She meant the water. Bodies dropped in would not be found.

The boat moved further from shore. No turning back.

"Amitabha," Won Jong chanted softly.

Yeon-shin gripped his sword.

"You don't seem afraid," Sword Art Flower said. "Youngster."

She was already holding hers.

"Shall we spill some blood first?"

Energy waves rose from her slender body. Her sword draw was swift -- proving the title. But the rising blade aimed not at Yeon-shin.

A woman bringing food widened her eyes. An innocent servant. Not part of the Deep Martial Alliance.

*Demonic path. Human lives are trivial to them.*

Yeon-shin was already circulating the Dynamic Technique. The wooden floor cracked under his step. His left hand pulled the serving woman gently aside. His right advanced.

The Desolate Sword struck out alongside anger rising like river mist. The Radiant Sword Style's light rays dyed the mist white, charging forward pure as a thunderbolt. It deflected Sword Art Flower's blade and still rushed on like a tremor to split her neck.

Blood.

*Stronger than even my own thoughts.*

He corrected his posture and ignored the rolling head.

"Don't try anything."

Those grabbing weapons in shock flinched. The sneers were gone, replaced by fear and panic. Sword Art Flower had been the strongest among them.

"She was the precious daughter of the Deep Martial Alliance's Cheng family!" the young master stammered. "Are you not afraid of consequences?"

"You're the ones who should worry about Desolate Sect's consequences. You aimed for our necks. For now, we go north. We'll decide your fate then."

He sheathed his sword. His face was indifferent.

"She was Sword Art Flower of the Cheng family! Do you truly not know?"

"We didn't exchange names."

He tapped his sword.

"Radiant Demon Wing. Lightning Flash."

The boat moved north through the river mist, and the young master's face went white as the wake behind them.
